description: Master catalogue of speech, fluency, and cognitive disorders

data.name: Rhotacism
data.description: Difficulty producing the /r/ phoneme; often substituted with /w/ or /l/.

data.name: Lambdacism
data.description: Difficulty producing the /l/ phoneme correctly.

data.name: Sigmatism
data.description: Lisping or distortion of sibilant sounds /s/ and /z/.

data.name: Voicing contrast errors
data.description: Confusion between voiced and voiceless cognate pairs (e.g. /p/-/b/, /t/-/d/, /k/-/g/).

data.name: Interdental fricative error
data.description: Substitution of /θ/ or /ð/ with /f/, /v/, /t/, or /d/.

data.name: Labiodental confusion
data.description: Confusion between /v/ and /w/ or /f/ and /p/.

data.name: Weak syllable deletion
data.description: Omission of unstressed syllables in multisyllabic words.

data.name: Stress pattern errors
data.description: Incorrect placement of lexical or sentence stress affecting intelligibility.

data.name: Reduced intelligibility
data.description: Overall speech clarity below functional threshold in connected speech.

data.name: Consonant cluster reduction
data.description: Simplification of consonant clusters (e.g. /str/ → /st/ or /tr/).

data.name: Stuttering
data.description: Disruptions in the forward flow of speech including repetitions, prolongations, and blocks.

data.name: Cluttering
data.description: Excessively rapid or irregular speech rate with reduced clarity.

data.name: Dysprosody
data.description: Abnormal prosodic patterns: disrupted rhythm, intonation, or stress.

data.name: Dysphonia
data.description: Voice quality disorder including breathiness, hoarseness, or pitch breaks.

data.name: Impaired breath support
data.description: Insufficient respiratory control for sustained phonation in connected speech.

data.name: Abnormal speech rate
data.description: Speech rate significantly above (tachylalia) or below (bradylalia) normal range.

data.name: Impaired pausing control
data.description: Inability to use syntactically appropriate pauses; either excessive or absent.

data.name: Flat intonation
data.description: Reduced pitch variation producing monotone speech.

data.name: Word retrieval difficulty
data.description: Difficulty accessing target lexical items during spontaneous speech (anomia).

data.name: Reduced discourse coherence
data.description: Difficulty maintaining topic or logical structure in extended utterances.

data.name: Pragmatic communication deficit
data.description: Impaired use of language in social/functional contexts (greetings, requests, turn-taking).

data.name: Reduced working memory for speech
data.description: Difficulty holding and producing longer utterances due to working memory limitations.

data.name: Impaired self-monitoring
data.description: Reduced ability to detect and self-correct errors in real time.
